incorrects.description: Student set one or more bars incorrectly on first attempt
Student placed one or more bars incorrectly on first attempt

steps.text: Check each bar carefully. Does the end line up with the value in the table?
Not quite. Check the data table and look at the axis lines carefully.

incorrects.description: Student set one or more bars incorrectly on second attempt
Student placed one or more bars incorrectly on second attempt

params.description: Ava bar position highlights on axis at 50.

steps.text: Ava is 50. That's right at the 50 line. The others are all between the lines. Which values are halfway between two lines?
Ava walked 50 miles. That's right at the 50 line. The others are between lines. Find where each bar ends by counting by 10s, then add 5 if it's halfway.

incorrects.description: Student set one or more bars incorrectly three or more times. System models the answer.
Student placed one or more bars incorrectly three or more times. System models the answer.

params.description: All four bars set in sequence: Kai=35, Ava=50, Noah=25, Zoe=45.
System demonstrates placing each bar: Kai at 35 (between 30 and 40), Ava at 50 (at the line), Noah at 25 (between 20 and 30), Zoe at 45 (between 40 and 50).

steps.text: Here's how to set each bar. Ava is 50, right at the line. Kai is 35, halfway between 30 and 40. Noah is 25, halfway between 20 and 30. Zoe is 45, halfway between 40 and 50. Each bar ends at its value, whether at a line or between. The graph shows all four: Kai=35, Ava=50, Noah=25, Zoe=45.

Let me show you. Kai walked 35 miles. That's between 30 and 40, halfway. Ava walked 50, right at the 50 line. Noah walked 25, halfway between 20 and 30. Zoe walked 45, halfway between 40 and 50. When a value ends in 5, it's always halfway between two lines on a scale of 10.
